title: Why do people say Shinji is a wimp
display_title: Why do people say Shinji is a wimp
wikitext:
[]: Why do people say Shinji is a wimp?             Quote Post by Sdat player » Wed Feb 13, 2013 3:26 pm It tires me, you see. That agument can pass by later in the series when he's depressed and with all the angst bottled up inside him, althought almost everyone with a passive persona would be like Shinji when in a state of depression similar to his. But what strikes me the most is when people agrue hes a wimp because he cant stand up to Asuka. Sure shes as messed up as him, and they do have a kind of romantic relationship where Asuka is a kudere or a tsundere and Shinji is seen as the guy who never raises his hand to her but the truth is, when he's not suicidal, he actually stands up to her without apologizing, like for example in ep 9 after they beat the angel and Asuka blames Shinji for the way the eva got on the floor, he says it was her fault. That is cannon evidence that he can hqve an assertive behaviour towards Asuka. Correct me if im wrong, but Shinji can man up to her.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Wed Feb 13, 2013 6:35 pm People say that Shinji is a wimp because they don't understand the series, they have the emotional range of a teaspoon, they never paid much attention to his actions, and they simply had no tolerance for his actions. Evangelion isn't a show where you can pass immediate judgement on the characters. One needs to be slightly more introspective when examining them; they need to be able to make the connection between their own emotions and the ones exhibited by the characters. Shinji's voice did get slightly annoying after a while, even in Japanese. My response? People need to get over it. There's no good reason to call him a wimp.

[]:             Quote Post by MAGI_01 » Wed Feb 13, 2013 7:00 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: People say that Shinji is a wimp because they don't understand the series, they have the emotional range of a teaspoon, they never paid much attention to his actions, and they simply had no tolerance for his actions. Evangelion isn't a show where you can pass immediate judgement on the characters. One needs to be slightly more introspective when examining them; they need to be able to make the connection between their own emotions and the ones exhibited by the characters. Shinji's voice did get slightly annoying after a while, even in Japanese. My response? People need to get over it. There's no good reason to call him a wimp.

[]:             Quote Post by Madonna » Wed Feb 13, 2013 8:00 pm I think a lot of it has to do with society's view of what a "man" is, usually they think of someone who is macho, bulky and doesn't cry. To me that's a really narrow and gender conformist view to have. I think Shinji despite his age is very courageous, and very far from a wimp, personally there are some other characters on the show (who have quite aggressive personalities) who are far more wimpish and cowardly than Shinji in my honest opinion.

[]:             Quote Post by Rj123541 » Wed Feb 13, 2013 8:30 pm As I will say to people who call Shinji a coward and so on, if they were in his position what would they do?

[]:             Quote Post by MAGI_01 » Wed Feb 13, 2013 8:32 pm You bring up a great point Madonna about society's views. Shinji is completely outside what society deems as "normal" as are the majority of the other characters. People pick up on that and since it does not fit their world view they instantly go on the attack.

[]:             Quote Post by Rj123541 » Wed Feb 13, 2013 8:33 pm Madonna wrote: I think a lot of it has to do with society's view of what a "man" is, usually they think of someone who is macho, bulky and doesn't cry. To me that's a really narrow and gender conformist view to have. I think Shinji despite his age is very courageous, and very far from a wimp, personally there are some other characters on the show (who have quite aggressive personalities) who are far more wimpish and cowardly than Shinji in my honest opinion.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Feb 13, 2013 8:34 pm Simply put, it's because he's so mired in self-hatred that he's unable to interact with others socially in any but the most superficial of fashions. People respond poorly to this, as they want to see him show some assertiveness and stand up for himself. And of course, they also tend to view the whole of his character through this lens, which is unfair; he can be quite brave when he knows what's expected of him and understands why he's doing what he's doing. But since their reaction is a visceral one it can be hard to change without some heavy self-reflection and such.

[]:             Quote Post by Atropos » Wed Feb 13, 2013 8:37 pm Shinji-hating appears to be in vogue again. I blame Q. P.S. If you want to see a thread with in-depth, textually supported defense of Shinji, see here .

[]:             Quote Post by caragnafog dog » Thu Feb 14, 2013 1:04 am Because they're impatient. Does confuse me a bit- surely they can sympathize with him on at least some level?

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Thu Feb 14, 2013 1:10 am Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: People say that Shinji is a wimp because they don't understand the series, they have the emotional range of a teaspoon, they never paid much attention to his actions, and they simply had no tolerance for his actions.

[]:             Quote Post by caragnafog dog » Thu Feb 14, 2013 1:14 am Well said.

[]:             Quote Post by BC Baron » Thu Feb 14, 2013 1:51 am Most people just seem to miss that it's not Shinji, but actually his father who's the biggest wimp in the entire show.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Thu Feb 14, 2013 3:47 am Oh man the circle-jerking. While most of the reasons people give for calling Shinji a wimp are mistaken, it doesn't exactly help that in many ways Shinji is indeed a wimp. While you can't really blame him for not reacting the best way to his situation, it still doesn't change the fact that he didn't act in the most functional or efficient way, and that ended up being costful or harmful to the people around him to a major or minor extent depending on which arc we're talking about. Asuka wouldn't have suffered so much if he wasn't an idiot, Misato wouldn't have to grieve alone if Shinji could have gone and hug her, maybe Touji wouldn't have lost his limbs if he decided to try and inmobilize Bardiel with the implied responsibility of harming him, maybe Rei and the Instrumentality project wouldn't have gone so badly if he kept a functional head. Now don't get me wrong, i really like Shinji, and in fact i hold a great admiration for the one i consider the greatest fictional character and (and in some ways hero) i have seen. I think the reason some people hate Shinji or call him a wimp is the same reason some people are pissed off or dislike Hisao Nakai: It is simply frustrating to see someone grappling or having difficultues with something that is obvious to you, and it can be downright infuriating when you see them letting that affect people or things you care about. EDIT: Also, while Gendo is certainly in many ways a wimp and the "villain" of NGE, it's worth noting that despite everything he gets shit done, and the happy ending Shinji achieved in both endings wouldn't have been without his (and Yui's and a few others's) work.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Thu Feb 14, 2013 4:03 am That's laughable. Expecting a kid that had zero parenting to do anything of the sort is utterly ridiculous. Nevermind the fact that everyone in the series acts like children based off Anno's belief Japan is a nation of children. There is no reason he should be expected to understand Asuka's contradictory behavior, no reason a kid should be expected to give emotional support to an adult. In fact the latter should be the other way around. This is a hilarious amount of nonsensical projection.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Feb 14, 2013 4:31 am Dream wrote: Asuka wouldn't have suffered so much if he wasn't an idiot, Misato wouldn't have to grieve alone if Shinji could have gone and hug her, maybe Touji wouldn't have lost his limbs if he decided to try and inmobilize Bardiel with the implied responsibility of harming him, maybe Rei and the Instrumentality project wouldn't have gone so badly if he kept a functional head.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Thu Feb 14, 2013 4:52 am Bagheera wrote: His ego was completely destroyed -- making him non-functional was the goal of the exercise.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Thu Feb 14, 2013 4:58 am Just to clarify, yeah i'm not surprised that Shinji reacted the way he did and how he got to that point in EoE. It is certainly understandable for a teenager to react so badly to such an horrible situation, but that doesn't make the situation any easier. It doesn't change the fact that it was possible for him things to have been done better, and i think that's what make some people dislike him. While most of Asuka's pain was self-inflicted, Shinji did serve as an enabler for her pain, thanks in no small part to his own issues. She would have been far less damaged if Shinji didn't react in some of the ways he did/reacted differently. And the fact he shouldn't be trying to comfort his own guarding doesn't change that was the necessary thing at the time (ironically i had two very similar experiences to that, one where i did the same thing Shinji did, in the other not, so believe me that i can understand how hard it must have been for Shinji and i can see why he didn't try to comfort her) It sucks and it's hard and painful, but that's the reality of the situation. I also would like to clarify that i don't blame Shinji for ending the world, as i can definitively see how he ended in the emotional and ideological/position where he decided for everything to die. I also would like to add that i don't hate Shinji, and at most i carry some irritation or frustration at the inefficient way he acted in some parts of the series, which caused more damaged than was necessary. How likely it is that someone would do better in his situation (almost certainly not an insecure teenager) is pretty arguable, but i doubt it's impossible. My issue is not with Shinji himself, but an irritation on the basis that things could have been done much better.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Thu Feb 14, 2013 5:21 am Which totally misses one of the meta points of the series, how does one learn to be an adult when all your role models still act like children? It was a cultural jab at Japan.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Feb 14, 2013 5:25 am Anonymous_Evafan wrote: It was being eroded but total collapse didn't happen until the fight with Asuka where readings started infinitely approaching zero or some such technobabble.

But what strikes me the most is when people agrue hes a wimp because he cant stand up

 it's not Shinji, but actually his father who's the biggest wimp in the entire show.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Thu Feb 14, 2013 3:47 am Oh man the circle-jerking. While most of the reasons people give for calling Shinji a wimp are mistaken, it doesn't exactly help that in many ways Shinji is indeed a wimp. While you can't really blame him for not reacting the best way to his situation, it still doesn't change the fact that he didn't act in the most functional or efficient way, and that ended up being costful or harmful to the people around him to a major or minor extent depending on which arc we're talking about. Asuka wouldn't have suffered so much if he wasn't an idiot, Misato wouldn't have to grieve alone if Shinji could have gone and hug her, maybe Touji wouldn't have lost his limbs if he decided to try and inmobilize Bardiel with the implied responsibility of harming him, maybe Rei and the Instrumentality project wouldn't have gone so badly if he kept a functional head. Now don't get me wrong, i really like Shinji, and in fact i hold a great admiration for the one i consider the greatest fictional character and (and in some ways hero) i have seen. I think the reason some people hate Shinji or call him a wimp is the same reason some people are pissed off or dislike Hisao Nakai: It is simply frustrating to see someone grappling or having difficultues with something that is obvious to you, and it can be downright infuriating when you see them letting that affect people or things you care about. EDIT: Also, while Gendo is certainly in many ways a wimp and the "villain" of NGE, it's worth noting that despite everything he gets shit done, and the happy ending Shinji achieved in both endings wouldn't have been without his (and Yui's and a few others's) work.
